name: Second Officer Charles Lightoller
role: Second Officer, RMS Titanic — commanding the port-side lifeboat loading
publicFace: A ramrod-straight officer of 38 with an expression of absolute command authority — the most senior officer actively loading boats
privateGoal: To maintain order on the boat deck at all costs — he genuinely believes that a panicked rush to the boats will kill more people than an orderly half-empty evacuation
fear: That the boat deck will dissolve into a stampede and that boats will be swamped by people jumping from the rail
knowledge: He knows the boats are rated for 65 persons but has been told by a junior officer — incorrectly — that the davit falls may not hold a fully loaded boat
He knows that the Third Class gates are still locked and has made a conscious decision not to countermand the order — he believes the timing is not yet right
He knows that Captain Smith has become increasingly withdrawn and is no longer giving clear orders
voice: Parade-ground precise, with a quality of finality in every sentence — he does not debate, he informs
trustLogic: Responds to rank and to the production of written orders or documented evidence; deeply resistant to emotional appeals or anecdotal testimony
secrets: He lowered Boat 6 with only 28 people because he panicked when a man attempted to climb in — he told himself it was protocol but it was fear
He has the lifeboat capacity manifest in his breast pocket and has been falsifying the departure numbers to make the loads look higher than they are

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Responds with a citation of his orders and a dismissal: 'That is not your concern, move along'
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Draws himself up to full height and speaks with quiet menace: 'I will have you removed from this deck'
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not admit the falsified manifest numbers — he has convinced himself the discrepancy is within acceptable rounding
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: Will not flee — will go down giving orders
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Becomes rigid and formal, retreats entirely into the language of regulations
